assignments.title: Introduction to NLP Quiz
assignments.description: Complete a 10-question multiple-choice quiz to assess your understanding of the fundamental concepts of Natural Language Processing, including text preprocessing, tokenization, and basic NLP tasks. The quiz will cover topics such as the definition of NLP, its applications, and the basic techniques used in the field.
requirements: Answer each question to the best of your ability
Choose the correct answer from the provided options
Submit your quiz for grading

assignments.title: Text Preprocessing Exercise
assignments.description: In this written exercise, you will practice text preprocessing techniques learned in the first week of the course. You will be provided with a sample text and will be required to perform the following tasks: (1) tokenize the text into individual words or tokens, (2) remove stop words, (3) apply stemming or lemmatization, and (4) remove any special characters or punctuation. Your submission should include the preprocessed text and a brief explanation of the steps you took to achieve the final result.
requirements: Tokenize the text into individual words or tokens
Remove stop words
Apply stemming or lemmatization
Remove any special characters or punctuation
Submit the preprocessed text with a brief explanation of the steps taken

assignments.title: Sentiment Analysis Project
assignments.description: For this assignment, you will implement a sentiment analysis project using a dataset of your choice. You should preprocess the text data, split it into training and testing sets, and train a machine learning model to classify the sentiment of the text as positive, negative, or neutral. You will then evaluate the performance of your model using metrics such as accuracy, precision, and recall.
requirements: Preprocess the text data by removing stop words, punctuation, and converting all text to lowercase
Split the preprocessed data into training and testing sets (80% for training and 20% for testing)
Train a machine learning model (such as logistic regression, decision tree, or random forest) to classify the sentiment of the text
Evaluate the performance of your model using metrics such as accuracy, precision, and recall
Compare your results to a baseline model (such as a simple majority classifier) to demonstrate the effectiveness of your approach
Submit a written report detailing your approach, results, and conclusions, including any visualizations or plots that help to illustrate your findings

assignments.title: Named Entity Recognition Quiz
assignments.description: For this assignment, you will complete a quiz to demonstrate your understanding of named entity recognition (NER) concepts. The quiz will cover key topics such as the definition and types of NER, its applications, and common techniques used in NER, including rule-based approaches and machine learning-based approaches. You will be required to answer a series of multiple-choice questions and short-answer questions. The quiz will be available on the course learning management system, and you will have 30 minutes to complete it.
requirements: Define named entity recognition and its importance in NLP
Identify and explain the different types of NER, including person, organization, and location
Describe the applications of NER in real-world scenarios, such as text classification and information extraction
Explain the common techniques used in NER, including rule-based approaches and machine learning-based approaches
Complete all questions within the allotted 30 minutes

assignments.title: Machine Translation Assignment
assignments.description: For this assignment, you will be working on a machine translation project. Your task is to develop a simple machine translation system using a deep learning model. You will be translating text from English to Spanish. You should use a pre-trained model and fine-tune it on a dataset of your choice. Your submission should include the code used to train and evaluate the model, as well as a brief report discussing the results and any challenges you faced.
requirements: Implement a machine translation system using a deep learning model
Use a pre-trained model and fine-tune it on a dataset of your choice
Translate text from English to Spanish
Include the code used to train and evaluate the model in your submission
Write a brief report discussing the results and any challenges you faced

assignments.title: Language Modeling Quiz
assignments.description: This quiz will assess your understanding of language modeling concepts, including the basics of language models, their applications, and evaluation metrics. You will be presented with a series of multiple-choice questions and will be required to select the correct answer for each question. The quiz will cover topics such as language model architectures, training objectives, and common language modeling tasks.
requirements: Read and understand the course materials related to language modeling
Review the notes and slides from the relevant lectures
Practice with sample questions and problems to assess your knowledge
Bring a calculator and pen/pencil for any calculations or note-taking
Allocate 60 minutes to complete the quiz

assignments.title: Data Preprocessing Project
assignments.description: In this assignment, you will work on a data preprocessing project. The goal is to preprocess a given dataset and apply various techniques to clean, transform, and prepare the data for analysis. You will be provided with a dataset and will be expected to perform the following tasks: (1) handle missing values, (2) remove duplicates, (3) perform data normalization, and (4) transform categorical variables into numerical variables. You will then submit a written report describing your approach, the techniques used, and the results obtained. The report should include visualizations of the data before and after preprocessing to demonstrate the effectiveness of your approach.
requirements: Handle missing values using appropriate techniques such as mean, median, or imputation
Remove duplicates to ensure data consistency
Perform data normalization to ensure all features are on the same scale
Transform categorical variables into numerical variables using techniques such as one-hot encoding or label encoding
Submit a written report describing the approach, techniques used, and results obtained
Include visualizations of the data before and after preprocessing

assignments.title: Data Visualization Quiz
assignments.description: For the Data Visualization Quiz, you are expected to demonstrate your understanding of data visualization techniques and their application in Natural Language Processing. The quiz will consist of multiple-choice questions and short-answer questions that test your knowledge of data visualization tools and methods. You will be given a set of sample data and will be asked to create visualizations using popular data visualization libraries such as Matplotlib or Seaborn. You will also be asked to interpret and analyze the visualizations, and to answer questions about the insights gained from the visualizations.
requirements: Understand the basics of data visualization
Familiarity with popular data visualization libraries such as Matplotlib or Seaborn
Ability to create visualizations using sample data
Ability to interpret and analyze visualizations
Ability to answer questions about the insights gained from the visualizations

assignments.title: NLP Libraries Comparison
assignments.description: For this assignment, you will compare and contrast three popular NLP libraries: NLTK, spaCy, and TensorFlow. Write a written report that includes the following: (1) an overview of each library, including its strengths and weaknesses, (2) a comparison of the libraries' performance on a specific NLP task, such as sentiment analysis or named entity recognition, (3) a discussion of the libraries' usability and ease of use, including any relevant APIs or interfaces, and (4) a conclusion that summarizes your findings and provides recommendations for which library to use in different scenarios.
requirements: Provide an overview of NLTK, spaCy, and TensorFlow, including their strengths and weaknesses
Compare the performance of the three libraries on a specific NLP task
Discuss the usability and ease of use of each library, including any relevant APIs or interfaces
Provide recommendations for which library to use in different scenarios
Support your comparison and recommendations with examples and evidence from the literature
Use proper citation and referencing throughout the report
Submit your report as a written document, with a minimum of 500 words and a maximum of 1000 words

assignments.title: NLTK Exercise
assignments.description: In this exercise, you will use the Natural Language Toolkit (NLTK) library to perform various tasks. The goal is to get familiar with the library and its functionalities. You will be working with text data and applying NLTK's tools to preprocess and analyze the text.
requirements: Install the NLTK library if you haven't done so already
Download the necessary NLTK data using the 'nltk.download()' function
Tokenize a given text into individual words or tokens
Remove stopwords from the tokenized text
Perform stemming or lemmatization on the text
Use NLTK's corpus readers to read and process text data
Complete all tasks as per the provided checklist

assignments.title: Text Classification Project
assignments.description: For this written assignment, you are expected to apply the concepts of text classification learned in the previous weeks to a real-world problem. You will be given a dataset of text samples and their corresponding labels, and you are required to develop a text classification model to classify new, unseen text samples into their respective categories. Your model should be trained and tested on the provided dataset, and you should evaluate its performance using appropriate metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. You should also discuss the challenges faced during the development of the model, the techniques used to overcome them, and the potential applications of the model in real-world scenarios.
requirements: Develop a text classification model using a suitable machine learning algorithm
Train and test the model on the provided dataset
Evaluate the performance of the model using metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score
Discuss the challenges faced during the development of the model and the techniques used to overcome them
Discuss the potential applications of the model in real-world scenarios
Submit a written report detailing the development and evaluation of the model, including any code used to implement the model

assignments.title: Information Retrieval Quiz
assignments.description: The Information Retrieval Quiz is designed to assess your understanding of the key concepts and techniques in information retrieval. You will be presented with a series of questions that require you to apply your knowledge of information retrieval models, algorithms, and evaluation metrics. The quiz will cover topics such as Boolean retrieval, vector space models, probabilistic models, and evaluation metrics such as precision, recall, and F1-score.
requirements: Answer all questions to the best of your ability
Use the concepts and techniques learned in the course to support your answers
Provide clear and concise explanations for each question
Use proper citation and referencing for any external sources used

assignments.title: Word Embeddings Assignment
assignments.description: For this assignment, you are expected to demonstrate your understanding of word embeddings by completing the following tasks: (1) Read and summarize the key concepts of word embeddings, including their types, such as Word2Vec and GloVe, (2) Choose a dataset of your choice and create word embeddings using a library such as Gensim, (3) Visualize and compare the word embeddings using dimensionality reduction techniques such as PCA or t-SNE, and (4) Write a short report (approximately 2-3 pages) discussing the results, challenges, and applications of word embeddings in NLP tasks.
requirements: Submit a written report in PDF format
Include a summary of key concepts of word embeddings
Provide code snippets for creating word embeddings using a library such as Gensim
Include visualizations of the word embeddings using dimensionality reduction techniques
Discuss the results, challenges, and applications of word embeddings in NLP tasks

assignments.title: Word2Vec Quiz
assignments.description: Complete a quiz to demonstrate understanding of Word2Vec, including its applications, advantages, and disadvantages. The quiz will consist of multiple-choice questions and short-answer questions that test your knowledge of Word2Vec fundamentals, such as vector space models, word embeddings, and similarity measures.
requirements: Understand the basics of Word2Vec, including CBOW and skip-gram models
Be able to explain the advantages and disadvantages of Word2Vec
Be able to apply Word2Vec to real-world problems, such as text classification and sentiment analysis
Complete all multiple-choice and short-answer questions to the best of your ability
Ensure that all answers are clear, concise, and well-organized

assignments.title: Recurrent Neural Networks Project
assignments.description: For this project, you will implement a recurrent neural network (RNN) to solve a text classification problem. You will be given a dataset of labeled text samples and will be required to preprocess the data, design and implement an RNN model, and evaluate its performance. The RNN model should be able to learn the patterns and relationships in the text data and make accurate predictions on unseen data.
requirements: Implement a recurrent neural network (RNN) using a library such as TensorFlow or PyTorch
Preprocess the text data by tokenizing the text, removing stop words, and converting the text to a numerical representation
Design and implement an RNN model architecture that is suitable for the text classification problem
Train the RNN model on the labeled dataset and evaluate its performance on a test dataset
Compare the performance of the RNN model with other machine learning models, such as support vector machines (SVMs) or random forests
Write a report that describes the RNN model, the dataset, the preprocessing steps, the training and evaluation process, and the results

assignments.title: LSTM Exercise
assignments.description: In this exercise, you will implement a basic LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) network using Python and the Keras library. Your task is to complete the code to build an LSTM model that can be used for text classification. The exercise is divided into several steps, and you will need to fill in the missing code in each step. The steps are: (1) import the necessary libraries, (2) load the dataset, (3) preprocess the data, (4) build the LSTM model, and (5) train the model. You will be provided with a checklist of tasks to complete in each step.
requirements: Import the necessary libraries, including Keras, NumPy, and Pandas
Load the dataset, which consists of text data and corresponding labels
Preprocess the data by tokenizing the text, removing stop words, and converting the text to numerical representations
Build the LSTM model by defining the input shape, adding LSTM layers, and compiling the model
Train the model using the preprocessed data and evaluate its performance using accuracy and loss metrics

assignments.title: Chatbots Project
assignments.description: For this project, you will design and implement a basic chatbot that can understand and respond to user queries. The chatbot should be able to perform the following tasks: greet the user, ask for the user's name, and respond to basic questions about the weather and time. You will use a natural language processing library such as NLTK or spaCy to process the user's input and generate responses. You will also use a machine learning model such as a neural network to train the chatbot on a dataset of example conversations.
requirements: Design and implement a chatbot that can understand and respond to user queries
Use a natural language processing library such as NLTK or spaCy to process user input
Use a machine learning model such as a neural network to train the chatbot on a dataset of example conversations
Implement the following tasks: greet the user, ask for the user's name, and respond to basic questions about the weather and time
Test and evaluate the chatbot's performance on a test dataset

assignments.title: Speech Recognition Quiz
assignments.description: For this speech recognition quiz, you will be presented with a series of audio recordings of spoken words or phrases. Your task is to accurately transcribe the audio recordings, taking into account any background noise, accents, or other factors that may affect the audio quality. You will be expected to demonstrate your understanding of speech recognition concepts, including acoustic modeling, language modeling, and decoding. You will have 30 minutes to complete the quiz, and you are allowed to use a dictionary or other reference materials, but you may not use any electronic devices or communicate with anyone during the quiz.
requirements: Accurately transcribe the audio recordings
Demonstrate understanding of speech recognition concepts
Complete the quiz within the allotted time
Use only allowed reference materials
Work independently without electronic devices or external communication

assignments.title: Evaluation Metrics Assignment
assignments.description: For this assignment, you will be responsible for understanding and applying various evaluation metrics used in Natural Language Processing (NLP). You will need to research and explain the differences between precision, recall, F1-score, and other relevant metrics. Additionally, you will be required to calculate these metrics for a given NLP task and analyze the results. Your submission should include a written report detailing your understanding of the metrics, their calculations, and the analysis of the results.
requirements: Research and explain the differences between precision, recall, and F1-score
Calculate these metrics for a given NLP task
Analyze the results and provide insights
Submit a written report detailing the understanding of the metrics, calculations, and analysis

assignments.title: Precision and Recall Quiz
assignments.description: In this quiz, students will be tested on their understanding of precision and recall in the context of Natural Language Processing. The quiz will cover topics such as the definition of precision and recall, how to calculate them, and their importance in evaluating the performance of NLP models. Students will be presented with a series of multiple-choice questions and will be expected to choose the correct answer for each question.
requirements: Define precision and recall and explain their importance in NLP
Calculate precision and recall given a set of true positives, false positives, and false negatives
Explain the difference between precision and recall and how they are used in evaluating the performance of NLP models
Identify the strengths and weaknesses of using precision and recall as evaluation metrics in NLP

assignments.title: Attention Mechanisms Project
assignments.description: In this written project, you will explore the concept of attention mechanisms in Natural Language Processing. You will be required to research and explain the self-attention mechanism, multi-head attention, and their applications in various NLP tasks. You should provide a detailed description of how attention mechanisms work, their advantages, and their limitations. Additionally, you will be asked to implement a simple attention mechanism using a popular deep learning library such as TensorFlow or PyTorch. Your project should include a brief introduction to attention mechanisms, a literature review of existing research, a description of your implementation, and a discussion of the results. The project should be well-organized, clearly written, and free of grammatical errors.
requirements: Research and explain the self-attention mechanism
Research and explain the multi-head attention mechanism
Implement a simple attention mechanism using a popular deep learning library
Provide a detailed description of how attention mechanisms work
Discuss the advantages and limitations of attention mechanisms
Include a brief introduction to attention mechanisms
Include a literature review of existing research
Include a description of your implementation
Include a discussion of the results
Ensure the project is well-organized and clearly written
Ensure the project is free of grammatical errors

assignments.title: Self-Attention Exercise
assignments.description: Implement a self-attention mechanism in a PyTorch model to demonstrate your understanding of the concept. The model should take a sequence of tokens as input and output a weighted sum of the input tokens based on the attention weights. You will need to define the Self-Attention class, initialize the weights, and implement the forward pass. The model should be trained on a simple dataset, such as a toy sequence classification task, to demonstrate the effectiveness of the self-attention mechanism.
requirements: Implement the Self-Attention class in PyTorch
Initialize the weights and define the forward pass
Train the model on a simple dataset
Visualize the attention weights to demonstrate the effectiveness of the self-attention mechanism
Submit your code and a brief report describing your implementation and results

assignments.title: Transfer Learning Assignment
assignments.description: For this written assignment, apply the concepts of transfer learning to a Natural Language Processing task. Choose a pre-trained model such as BERT, RoBERTa, or XLNet, and fine-tune it for a specific task like sentiment analysis, named entity recognition, or text classification. Write a report detailing your approach, including the model selection, dataset choice, training parameters, and evaluation metrics. Compare the performance of your fine-tuned model with the baseline performance of the pre-trained model. Submit your report, along with your code and any relevant visualizations or results.
requirements: Choose a pre-trained model and NLP task
Select a suitable dataset for fine-tuning
Implement fine-tuning with appropriate training parameters
Evaluate the performance of the fine-tuned model
Compare with baseline performance of the pre-trained model
Submit a written report with code, visualizations, and results

assignments.title: Pre-trained Models Quiz
assignments.description: Complete a quiz to demonstrate understanding of pre-trained models, including their applications, advantages, and limitations. The quiz will cover key concepts such as model fine-tuning, transfer learning, and the use of pre-trained models for various NLP tasks. Students will be expected to answer multiple-choice questions, short-answer questions, and provide examples of how pre-trained models can be used in real-world scenarios.
requirements: Understand the concept of pre-trained models and their applications in NLP
Be able to explain the advantages and limitations of using pre-trained models
Demonstrate knowledge of popular pre-trained models such as BERT, RoBERTa, and XLNet
Provide examples of how pre-trained models can be fine-tuned for specific NLP tasks
Answer multiple-choice and short-answer questions to demonstrate understanding of pre-trained models

assignments.title: Adversarial Attacks Project
assignments.description: In this project, you will explore the concept of adversarial attacks in Natural Language Processing (NLP). Your task is to design and implement an adversarial attack on a pre-trained NLP model. You will start by selecting a pre-trained model and a dataset, and then you will attempt to craft adversarial examples that can fool the model. You will also evaluate the effectiveness of your attack and discuss the implications of your findings.
requirements: Select a pre-trained NLP model and a dataset
Design and implement an adversarial attack on the model
Evaluate the effectiveness of the attack
Discuss the implications of the findings
Submit a written report of the project, including code and results

assignments.title: Adversarial Training Quiz
assignments.description: This quiz assesses the student's understanding of adversarial training in the context of Natural Language Processing. The quiz will cover the concepts of adversarial attacks, adversarial training, and defending against attacks. Students are expected to demonstrate their knowledge of how to train models to be robust against adversarial examples and how to evaluate the effectiveness of adversarial training methods. The quiz will include multiple-choice questions, short-answer questions, and a case study that requires students to apply their knowledge of adversarial training to a real-world scenario.
requirements: Define adversarial training and its importance in NLP
Explain the difference between adversarial attacks and adversarial training
Describe the process of generating adversarial examples
Discuss the methods for defending against adversarial attacks
Evaluate the effectiveness of adversarial training methods using metrics such as accuracy and robustness
Apply adversarial training to a real-world NLP task, such as text classification or sentiment analysis

assignments.title: Social Media Analysis Assignment
assignments.description: For this assignment, you will be required to analyze a social media platform of your choice (e.g., Twitter, Facebook, Instagram) and perform sentiment analysis on a specific topic or hashtag. You will need to collect a dataset of social media posts, preprocess the data, and then apply a sentiment analysis algorithm to determine the overall sentiment of the posts. You should also explore topic modeling techniques to identify underlying themes and trends in the data. Your analysis should include visualizations and discussions of your findings, as well as an evaluation of the limitations and potential biases of your approach.
requirements: Collect and preprocess a dataset of social media posts
Apply a sentiment analysis algorithm to the dataset
Perform topic modeling to identify underlying themes and trends
Create visualizations to illustrate your findings
Discuss the limitations and potential biases of your approach
Submit a written report detailing your analysis and results

assignments.title: Sentiment Analysis Exercise
assignments.description: In this sentiment analysis exercise, you will apply the concepts learned in week 14 to analyze the sentiment of a given text. You will use a checklist to evaluate the sentiment of the text based on the presence of positive, negative, or neutral keywords. Your task is to read the text carefully, identify the keywords that indicate the sentiment, and check the corresponding boxes in the checklist. You should also provide a brief explanation for your answer, stating why you think the text has a certain sentiment.
requirements: Read the given text carefully
Identify the keywords that indicate the sentiment
Check the corresponding boxes in the checklist
Provide a brief explanation for your answer
Submit your completed checklist as a written assignment

assignments.title: Clinical Text Analysis Project
assignments.description: For this project, you will be working on clinical text analysis. Your task is to analyze a given set of clinical notes and identify the most relevant information, such as patient history, diagnosis, treatment, and medication. You will need to preprocess the text data by tokenizing the text, removing stop words, and stemming or lemmatizing the words. Then, you will apply a named entity recognition (NER) technique to extract relevant entities such as diseases, medications, and medical procedures. Finally, you will need to evaluate the performance of your model using metrics such as precision, recall, and F1-score.
requirements: Analyze a given set of clinical notes
Preprocess the text data by tokenizing the text, removing stop words, and stemming or lemmatizing the words
Apply a named entity recognition (NER) technique to extract relevant entities
Evaluate the performance of your model using metrics such as precision, recall, and F1-score
Submit a written report detailing your approach, results, and conclusions

assignments.title: Medical Question Answering Quiz
assignments.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of medical question answering concepts, including clinical text analysis and information retrieval techniques. You will be presented with a set of questions that require you to apply your knowledge of medical terminology, disease diagnosis, and treatment options. The quiz consists of multiple-choice questions and short-answer questions, and you are expected to provide concise and accurate responses. You will have 60 minutes to complete the quiz, and it is recommended that you review the topics covered in weeks 13-15 before attempting the quiz.
requirements: Review of clinical text analysis concepts
Understanding of medical terminology and disease diagnosis
Familiarity with information retrieval techniques
Ability to provide concise and accurate responses
Completion of the quiz within the allotted 60 minutes

assignments.title: Final Project
assignments.description: For the final project, you will apply the concepts and techniques learned throughout the course to a real-world problem in Natural Language Processing. You will design, develop, and evaluate a project that demonstrates your understanding of NLP concepts, including text preprocessing, sentiment analysis, named entity recognition, machine translation, and language modeling. Your project should include a written report that describes your approach, methodology, and results, as well as any relevant code or data. The project should be a comprehensive application of NLP techniques to a real-world problem, and should demonstrate your ability to think critically and creatively about NLP applications.
requirements: Clearly define a real-world problem in NLP and propose a solution
Design and develop a project that applies NLP concepts and techniques to the problem
Evaluate the project and report on the results, including any challenges or limitations
Submit a written report that describes the project, including the approach, methodology, and results
Include any relevant code or data with the report

assignments.title: Final Project Presentation
assignments.description: For the final project presentation, you are expected to submit a written report that summarizes your project's key findings, methodology, and contributions to the field of Natural Language Processing. The report should be 5-7 pages in length, double-spaced, and include the following sections: introduction, literature review, methodology, results, discussion, and conclusion. You should also include any relevant visual aids, such as tables, figures, or diagrams, to support your presentation. The presentation should demonstrate your understanding of the course material and your ability to apply NLP concepts to a real-world problem.
requirements: Submit a written report that summarizes your project's key findings, methodology, and contributions
Include an introduction, literature review, methodology, results, discussion, and conclusion
Include relevant visual aids, such as tables, figures, or diagrams
Demonstrate your understanding of the course material and your ability to apply NLP concepts to a real-world problem
Adhere to the 5-7 page length and double-spaced formatting requirements
